comments.author: Mannat Guliani
comments.text:
HN:
Aurangabad – 11 digits
Dibrugarh - Serial number/Year
GMCH Chandigarh – 9-11 digits
IOG Chennai – 4-6 digits

comments.author: Mannat Guliani
comments.text:
HN:
This field should appear as MRD number for mother in Aurangabad – 4/5/6 digits
11 digit admission number assigned to mother in Dibrugarh
Can be autofilled from maternal UID for GMCH Chd and IOG Chennai
Mandatory field for PGI – 10 digits

comments.author: Mannat Guliani
comments.text:
HN:
This field should appear as MRD number for baby in Aurangabad – 4/5/6 digits
Dibrugarh – admission number only assigned to baby if admitted in the NICU (11 digits)
9-11 digit number for GMCH Chandigarh generated 2 mins after birth if baby survives
Mandatory field for PGI – 10 digits
IOG Chennai – same as UID 4-6 digits

comments.author: Mannat Guliani
comments.text: HN: This REDCap should only appear for PGI in all forms

comments.author: Mannat Guliani
comments.text:
HN:
No Annual number for Aurangabad
Delivery room Logbook serial number – Dibrugarh
PGI – 4 digits
SNCU – 4 digit number in IOG Chennai

comments.author: Mannat Guliani
comments.text:
HN: Address Format at other sites:
Dibrugarh - C/O, village, Post Office, Police Station, District
Aurangabad, GMCH Chd – Pick address from Aadhar card

comments.text:
HN: With + options to enter value of each feed volume and then should be auto calculated to get total volume of the feed per day, and then divided by the weight of the day to get an average for the day.
If weight is less than 1 kg , divide by 1, if more, than value
